_changed: 2026-07-09: tier.roundtrip_ship_insured (per-card scalar) removed. Shipping now lives at SERVICE level as band-keyed per-order cost, because (a) both graders bill per shipment, not per card, and (b) the Beckett wizard showed identical insurance/return at Base and Standard, so shipping keys off declared value, not tier.
_rule: No number enters this file without a verified_on date. Unverified stays null and triggers REFUSED.

shipping._transit_note: [confirm/fill] PSA's clock starts at INTAKE ('turnaround does not begin until the order is entered'), so transit is on top, same as Beckett. Previously set to 0 in error.

bands.source: PSA Online Submission Center, Submission Summary, Regular, 1 item, $250 declared

shipping._transit_note: [confirm/fill] Beckett quotes BUSINESS days excluding transit, so this is required, not cosmetic.

bands.source: Beckett wizard, Order Summary, 1 card. Identical at Base and Standard, so band keys on declared value, not tier.
bands._outbound_note: [confirm/fill] You ship to Dallas yourself. Until outbound is entered, BGS totals are NOT round trips.

base._note: Listed as '75+'. Treat as a floor. Fee is with subgrades; $14.95 without, plus $3 upcharge for any 10s when subgrades are added.

standard._note: With subgrades. 45 business days ~= 63 calendar days, plus transit.

tag._status: dormant — added 2026-07-09 as a modeled option, not an active lane.
tag._why_dormant: PSA Value pause spilled into TAG: Basic/Standard/Express sold out, leaving only Priority ($149) and Walk-Through ($299). Too expensive and too scarce to be the cheap lane it looks like on paper. Revisit if lower tiers reopen.
tag._eligibility: [verify] Mainly licensed cards 1989+ and up to 50pt. Yu-Gi-Oh is often the 'PSA is more practical' side; Kayou eligibility UNCONFIRMED. Check before assuming a card can even go to TAG.
tag._resale_caveat: TAG slabs resell ~35-60% below PSA for Pokemon, wider on vintage (multiple sources, 2026). Model as its OWN graded-price node; a lower fee does NOT mean a higher net. Same trap as BGS.

shipping._note: [confirm/fill] Not quoted. TAG lists a fixed submission kit (~$49.95) separate from per-card fees — capture it when pulling a real quote.

priority._note: [confirm/fill] Listed $149 / 15-30 business days as of late June 2026, not pulled from a live checkout. Only orderable tier right now.

graded_price_guard._note: BGS grades harder: a BGS 9.5 often reflects what PSA calls a 10, and for Pokemon a 9.5 can trade below raw. Feeding PSA comps to a BGS submission hands BGS a win it never earned. graded_prices_service on the card must equal grading.service.

ebay._category: Toys & Hobbies > Collectible Card Games (also Non-Sport Trading Cards, Sports Trading Cards)
ebay._note: 13.25% up to $7,500 per item; 2.35% above. Per-order fee $0.40 over $10, $0.30 at or under $10.
ebay._base_warning: eBay's FVF base = item price + buyer-paid shipping + SALES TAX. Offering free shipping keeps shipping out of the base; the tax portion is unavoidable and adds roughly 0.8pp of effective drag on a ~6% tax. Model conservatively or raise pct.

rnv_shop._note: Stripe standard US domestic card; Payment Links use standard rates. Disputes cost $15 each, win or lose, and are NOT modeled here. You are seller of record: DC sales tax is yours to collect and remit, unlike eBay's marketplace-facilitator handling.
